title: prenanthes serpentaria var. nana
intro: Prenanthes serpentaria var. nana, also known as Dwarf White Snakeroot, is a variety of flowering plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and snake bites, due to its toxicity from the alkaloid seselin. The health benefits of Prenanthes serpentaria var. nana include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and reducing pain and swelling. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, have been used in folk medicine, but caution is advised due to its potential toxicity.
